media.altText: Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey in natural habitat

sections.subtitle: Snub-nosed monkeys in Tibetan Highlands
sections.text: Pickup from Lijiang and travel to Baima Snow Mountain. Witness rare Black Snub-nosed Monkeys in their natural habitat, learn about biodiversity conservation with expert guides, and experience authentic countryside life. Embark on an unforgettable journey into Shangri-La's pristine wilderness. Discover breathtaking landscapes from vibrant wetlands to ancient forests, and immerse yourself in the rich cultures of Tibetan and Lisu minorities.

items.title: What's Special
items.subtitle: Discover endangered primates
items.highlight: Black Snub-nosed Monkeys and biodiversity conservation

items.title: Duration
items.subtitle: Two Days
items.highlight:
Day 1: 9:00 AM to 9:00 PM
Day 2: 8:00 AM to 9:00 PM

items.title: Location
items.subtitle: Lijiang & Shangri-La
items.highlight: Baima Snow Mountain Nature Reserve | Altitudes 1,800-2,800m

items.title: Activity Level
items.subtitle: Moderate
items.highlight: Hiking: No more than 8-9 km per day

items.title: Monkey Encounter
items.subtitle: Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkeys
items.text: Experience the magic of observing Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkeys in their pristine natural mountain habitat. Witness their daily life unfold and meet the dedicated conservation rangers who protect these incredible primates.

image.altText: Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey in natural habitat

items.title: Cultural Immersion
items.subtitle: Tibetan and Lisu Villages
items.text: Experience the vibrant cultures of Tibetan and Lisu ethnic minorities in their quaint villages and hamlets. Explore unique customs, admire distinctive traditional architecture, feel their warm hospitality, and savor authentic local cuisine.

items.title: Conservation Bureau
items.subtitle: Baima Snow Mountain
items.text: Gain exclusive insights into local conservation efforts with a visit to the Baima Snow Mountain Nature Conservation Bureau. Learn about the vital work being done to protect the rich biodiversity of this region.

items.title: Panoramic Views
items.subtitle: First Bend of the Yangtze
items.text: Embark on a rewarding short hike to panoramic viewpoints overlooking the legendary First Bend of the Yangtze River and the picturesque Shigu town. Capture iconic photographs of Yunnan's most breathtaking landscapes.

image.altText: First Bend of Yangtze River panorama

items.title: Alpine Wetlands
items.subtitle: Lashi Nature Reserve
items.text: Discover a breathtaking haven for migratory waterbirds at Lashi Wetlands Nature Reserve. Explore this rare alpine wetland ecosystem and learn about crucial conservation efforts underway to protect this precious environment.

items.title: Historical Market
items.subtitle: Shigu Naxi Heritage
items.text: Immerse yourself in the vibrant atmosphere of Shigu Naxi Minority Town's bustling market with fresh produce brought by villagers from the Laojun Mountains. Uncover Ming Dynasty history etched into an ancient stone monument.

items.title: Monkey Exhibition
items.subtitle: Learn About the Species
items.text: Gain a comprehensive introduction to the endangered Yunnan snub-nosed monkey at a dedicated exhibition. Learn about their unique characteristics, fascinating social behaviors, and critical conservation initiatives.

items.title: Sustainable Beekeeping
items.subtitle: Himalayan Bee Farm
items.text: Visit a Himalayan bee farm and meet a passionate local beekeeper. Delve into the fascinating world of these unique bees, learn about traditional beekeeping practices, and understand the link between conservation and sustainable development.

items.title: Ancient Forests
items.subtitle: Primeval Forest Exploration
items.text: Embark on an unforgettable hike through the ancient primeval forests of Baima Snow Mountain Nature Reserve. Explore astonishing biodiversity and discover rare endemic plant and animal species in Yunnan's wilderness.

image.altText: Ancient primeval forest in Baima Mountains

items.title: Citizen Science
items.subtitle: Wildlife Monitoring
items.text: Become a citizen scientist and wildlife explorer! Learn to use state-of-the-art infrared cameras for biodiversity monitoring and actively participate in setting them up. Experience the thrill of capturing images of elusive nocturnal animals.

items.title: Expert Guides
items.subtitle: Conservation Professionals
items.text: Benefit from the expertise of a knowledgeable English-speaking guide throughout your ecotour. Meet and interact with passionate conservation professionals, learning firsthand about their crucial on-the-ground work.

items.title: Rural Life
items.subtitle: Countryside Experience
items.text: Immerse yourself in the authentic traditional countryside lifestyle of local Tibetan and Lisu minorities. Observe time-honored farming and pasturing practices, gaining a deeper appreciation for their intimate connection to the land.

activities: Begin with a pickup from your Lijiang hotel and head to the pristine Lashi Wetlands, where misty mornings awaken thousands of migratory birds. Watch the sunrise paint the wetlands in golden hues as elegant waterbirds glide across mirror-still waters. Continue your journey and hike to breathtaking viewpoints overlooking the legendary First Bend of the Yangtze River.

After a local lunch, explore Shigu Naxi Minority Town market and uncover Ming Dynasty history. After this quick stop, travel along the picturesque Yangtze River passing through serene minority villages where time seem to have slowed down. In the late afternoon, visit a Himalayan bee farm to learn about traditional beekeeping and experience first-hand the day-to-day village life.  Before the sunset, join a short hike with a local guide to the sacred forest spring and discover the local community’s deep relationship with nature.
As twilight descends, savor authentic local cuisine, then settle into your mountain retreat where stars emerge in crystal-clear skies.

Awaken to crisp mountain air and venture into the heart of Baima Snow Mountain Nature Reserve. Embark on an enchanting trek through biodiverse ancient forests to encounter the elusive Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkeys in their natural habitat and meet the passionate monkey conservation rangers.

After a delicious lunch at a trout fish farm, visit the Baima Snow Mountain Nature Conservation Bureau for exclusive insights into ecosystem conservation work, then explore a dedicated Black Snub-nosed Monkey exhibition. Set out for a journey back to Lijiang.

After arriving back in Lijiang, enjoy a hearty traditional feast of Yunnan delicacies and return to your hotel as the sun sets, carrying memories of an extraordinary encounter with China's incredible biodiversity.

contactNote.text: For groups of 1 person or more than 6 people

included.text: English-Speaking Coordinator/Guide (Lynne Lyu or Lukasz Madrzynski), Local Guides, Entrance Tickets to the National Park, Traditional Local Food (Breakfasts, Lunches, Dinners), Comfortable Accommodation, Private Transportation, Outdoor Travel Insurance.
included.notIncluded: Personal Expenses (Souvenirs, etc.), Voluntary Gratuities for the Coordinators/Guides
included.meetingPoint: Pickup: 9:00 AM at your Lijiang hotel | Return: Approximately 9:00 PM to your Lijiang hotel

sections.subtitle: Click on a question to reveal the answer

items.question: What should I bring?
items.answer: We recommend comfortable hiking shoes, layers for changing temperatures, sun protection, a water bottle, raincoat or umbrella for rainy days, and your camera.

items.question: Is this suitable for children?
items.answer: Yes! This tour is family-friendly. Children under 12 should be accompanied by an adult. The moderate hiking distance (8-9 km/day) is achievable for most fitness levels.

items.question: What's the cancellation policy?
items.answer: Full refund if cancelled 7+ days before the tour. 50% refund for 3-6 days. No refund within 48 hours.

items.question: What happens if it rains?
items.answer: Most activities continue in light rain. For safety during heavy storms, we'll adjust the itinerary to indoor cultural experiences and conservation center visits.

items.question: Are there any physical requirements?
items.answer: Basic fitness is recommended. You'll hike up to 9 km per day on mountain trails. The terrain is generally gentle but at altitude (up to 2,800m), pace will be slower.

items.question: How do I get to the meeting point?
items.answer: We provide pickup from any hotel in Lijiang Old Town area. If you're staying outside this area, please contact us to arrange an alternative meeting point.

items.question: What language do the guides speak?
items.answer: We are fluent in English, Polish and Chinese.

items.question: How does this trip support conservation and sustainable development?
items.answer: Each booking directly supports local conservation efforts and community development. We partner with nature reserves for wildlife monitoring, employ local Naxi, Lisu, and Tibetan guides and community hosts, and are actively involved in local environmental protection and sustainable development initiatives.
